title: The Desert Door
lines: The tunnel terminates abruptly at a simple, heavy wooden door of weathered oak. It stands alone in the absolute, stygian darkness, anchored to nothing but the void itself—a solitary portal between disparate and dying realities.
Through the keyhole, you hear the howl of a cyclopean wind—a freezing, primordial gale—that carries the scent of ancient, sun-scorched sand and the dry, rattling whisper of an endless, orange desert.

options.desc: 1. Turn the cold brass handle and step through the door
2. Press your ear against the keyhole and listen to the void
3. Kick the door open with all your desperate strength
